message.content:
I need to update the readme to include info on the SDK package. here is a copy paste of what may be relevant:Prerequisites (both interfaces)
Python 3.10 or higher

An Anthropic API key (get one here)

The repository cloned locally

bash
git clone https://github.com/ChaunceyCon/CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent.git
cd CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent
Option 1 — Web Chat Interface (Flask)
The web interface runs in your browser at localhost:5000.

Install dependencies:

bash
pip install flask anthropic
Set your API key:

bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key-here
Start the server:

bash
python web_coach.py
Open in browser:

text
http://localhost:5000/
Use the About Me panel (tab on the right side) to enter your name, age, neurodivergent conditions, and background before starting a chat. This lets the coach personalize responses to your situation. Fill it out and click Save profile before sending your first message.

Option 2 — Claude Agent SDK (Terminal/CLI)
The CLI version runs directly in your terminal using the Claude Agent SDK.

Install the SDK (Claude Code CLI is bundled automatically — no separate install needed ):

bash
pip install claude-agent-sdk anthropic
Set your API key:

bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key-here
Run the coach:

bash
python coach.py
You will be prompted to type your opening message. The agent reads your .claude/skills/ files and routes to the right skill automatically.Example opening messages:

text
What's on your mind? → I have a paper due tomorrow and I keep reading the
prompt but I do not understand what the professor actually wants from me.

What's on your mind? → I am so tired. I have been pushing through everything
for weeks and I do not know how much longer I can keep doing this.
Type exit at any point to end the session.

Option 3 — Original Claude Code Terminal (still works)
bash
From the repo root in WSL/terminal
claude
Then paste the session-start prompt format from the Skills Overview section above.

Persona Files (for testing)
Test personas are in data/personas/. Reference a persona name in your opening message (e.g. "I am Maya...") to simulate a realistic student profile.

Persona    Profile
maya.json    Sophomore, ADHD, two jobs, chronically behind
david.json    Senior CS, autistic, heavy masker
priya.json    Junior nursing, high-achieving, burning out
jordan.json    Freshman, first-gen, dyslexic
chauncey.json    CS junior, self-testing persona
One thing to check before adding this to your README: confirm that coach.py is actually saved in your repo root. If it was only provided in our chat but never saved, let me know and I will regenerate the complete file for you to drop in.
﻿
KilldroneX
killdronex

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- burnout-check: Helps users recognize and interrupt masking fatigue, burnout, and over-accommodation patterns. Reflects back what the user has described, asks how they are actually doing beyond performance and grades, and treats rest and recovery as legitimate strategies — not last resorts. Does not push users to keep going when they need to stop.
- session-start: Activates at the start of every conversation. Reads the user's opening message, classifies their intent, and routes to the correct skill. Asks exactly one clarifying question if intent is ambiguous before routing. Never gives advice before understanding what the user actually needs.
- situation-decoder: Helps users decode ambiguous academic or social situations: confusing assignment instructions, unclear rubric language, cryptic professor feedback, and socially demanding contexts like presentations, group projects, or advisor meetings. Uses Socratic questions to surface what the user already understands before adding interpretation.
- strategy-toolkit: Recommends a targeted set of strategies for a specific academic or organizational challenge. Always asks about context (environment, energy, time) before recommending anything. Prioritizes non-AI approaches and treats body-doubling, environmental changes, interest-based reframing, and peer strategies as first-class options alongside any tech tools.
- strength-mapper: Translates abstract self-knowledge into one concrete adjusted approach for the specific challenge in front of the user right now. Takes what the user already knows about how they work and applies it to the current situation. Produces one actionable thing, not a general plan.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Neurodiversity Support Agent
2	
3	An AI agent prototype that helps neurodivergent college students turn abstract self-knowledge into concrete situated action. Built as a Claude Code skills-based prototype for COS 498/598: Generative AI Agents, Spring 2026 at the University of Maine.
4	
5	The agent acts as a collaborative thought partner — warm, specific, and non-patronizing. It helps students decode confusing assignments, apply what they know about how they work, find practical strategies, and recognize burnout before it becomes a crisis.
6	
7	***
8	
9	## What This Agent Does
10	
11	- **Decodes confusing academic situations** — assignment prompts, rubric language, professor feedback, and socially demanding events like presentations or advisor meetings
12	- **Maps self-knowledge to situated action** — takes what you know about how you work and applies it to the specific challenge in front of you right now
13	- **Recommends a full strategy toolkit** — prioritizes non-AI approaches like body-doubling, interest-layer reframing, environmental anchoring, and peer strategies
14	- **Checks for burnout** — helps you recognize masking fatigue and over-accommodation, and treats rest as a legitimate option, not a last resort
15	
16	***
17	
18	## Web Version
19	
20	- Access the web version of the Agent at https://co-4-2-prototype-class-project-agent-production.up.railway.app/
21	- If you wish to run the agent on your own device instead, continue below.
22	
23	## Prerequisites
24	
25	- VS Code with the Claude Code extension installed and authenticated
26	- An Anthropic API key or Claude Max subscription
27	- This repository cloned locally and opened in VS Code
28	- Entire CLI enabled (`entire enable` from the repo root in WSL/terminal)
29	
30	***
31	
32	## Setup
33	
34	```bash
35	# Clone the repository
36	git clone https://github.com/ChaunceyCon/CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent.git
37	cd CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent
38	
39	# Enable Entire (requires WSL or Linux terminal)
40	entire enable
41	
42	# Start Claude Code
43	claude
44	```
45	
46	***
47	
48	## How to Run the Agent
49	
50	### Step 1: Start a fresh Claude Code session
51	
52	Open a new terminal session and run:
53	
54	```bash
55	claude
56	```
57	
58	Make sure **Superpowers plugin is disabled** if you have it installed. Run `/plugin` inside Claude Code and verify it is toggled off. If superpowers is active, it will intercept the skills and the agent will not work correctly.
59	
60	### Step 2: Paste the starter prompt
61	
62	Copy and paste the following into Claude Code, replacing the user message in quotes with your own situation:
63	
64	```
65	Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
66	use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
67	from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
68	YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
69	TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.
70	
71	"[Write your opening message here — see example prompts below]"
72	```
73	
74	### Step 3: Continue the conversation naturally
75	
76	After the agent responds, keep replying as yourself. The agent will route between skills automatically based on what you need.
77	
78	***
79	
80	## Example Opening Prompts
81	
82	Try one of these to get started, or write your own:
83	
84	**If you are confused by an assignment:**
85	```
86	Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
87	use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
88	from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
89	YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
90	TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.
91	
92	"I have a paper due tomorrow and I keep reading the prompt but
93	I do not understand what the professor actually wants from me."
94	```
95	
96	**If you are struggling to start something:**
97	```
98	Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
99	use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
100	from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
101	YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
102	TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.
103	
104	"I have a big assignment this weekend but it feels pointless and
105	I cannot make myself start it. I know how I work but I do not
106	know how to apply that here."
107	```
108	
109	**If you are exhausted and burning out:**
110	```
111	Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
112	use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
113	from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
114	YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
115	TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.
116	
117	"I am so tired. I have been pushing through everything for weeks
118	and I do not know how much longer I can keep doing this."
119	```
120	
121	**If you want strategies for a specific situation:**
122	```
123	Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
124	use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
125	from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
126	YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
127	TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.
128	
129	"I have a group presentation next week and I am dreading it.
130	I need strategies that actually fit how I work, not generic advice."
131	```
132	
133	***
134	
135	## Skills Overview
136	
137	| Skill | What It Does |
138	|---|---|
139	| `session-start` | Reads your opening message and routes to the right skill. Asks one clarifying question if needed. |
140	| `situation-decoder` | Decodes confusing assignments, rubrics, professor feedback, and social situations step by step. |
141	| `strength-mapper` | Translates what you know about yourself into one concrete adjusted approach for today's challenge. |
142	| `strategy-toolkit` | Recommends targeted strategies based on your environment, energy, and time. Non-AI approaches first. |
143	| `burnout-check` | Helps you recognize masking fatigue and burnout. Offers rest as a real option, not a last resort. |
144	
145	***
146	
147	## Persona Files
148	
149	Test personas are available in `data/personas/` to help simulate realistic interactions:
150	
151	- `maya.json` — Sophomore with ADHD, working two jobs, chronically behind
152	- `david.json` — Senior CS student, autistic, heavy masker, struggles with ambiguous instructions
153	- `priya.json` — Junior in nursing, high-achieving but burning out from over-accommodation
154	- `jordan.json` — Freshman, first-gen, dyslexic, avoids asking for help
155	- `chauncey.json` — CS junior persona for self-testing
156	
157	To use a persona in testing, reference the persona name in your opening message (e.g., "I am Maya...") so the agent can read the corresponding file and personalize its responses.
158	
159	***
160	
161	## Repository Structure
162	
163	```
164	.claude/
165	  skills/
166	    session-start/SKILL.md
167	    situation-decoder/SKILL.md
168	    strength-mapper/SKILL.md
169	    strategy-toolkit/SKILL.md
170	    burnout-check/SKILL.md
171	data/
172	  personas/          — JSON persona files for testing
173	  interactions/
174	    actual-logs/     — Real test conversation logs
175	docs/
176	  neurodiversity-agent-design.md   — Full design document
177	eval/
178	  evaluate.py        — Evaluation functions
179	  run_eval.py        — Evaluation runner script
180	  sample_conversations.json
181	  results/           — Per-conversation evaluation output
182	experiments/
183	  eval-structural-results.md
184	  maya-test-1-notes.md
185	  chauncey-test-1-notes.md
186	  chauncey-test-2-notes.md
187	```
188	
189	***
190	
191	## Running Evaluations
192	
193	From the repo root in WSL:
194	
195	```bash
196	# Structural metrics only (no API key needed)
197	python3 eval/run_eval.py --precomputed > experiments/eval-structural-results.md
198	
199	# Full interactive evaluation
200	python3 eval/run_eval.py
201	```
202	
203	***
204	
205	## Design Principles
206	
207	- The agent asks questions rather than lectures
208	- Non-AI strategies (body-doubling, environmental anchoring, peer approaches) are always offered first
209	- The agent never diagnoses, labels, or pathologizes the user
210	- Rest and stopping are treated as legitimate options, not last resorts
211	- If a user expresses a crisis, the agent provides 988 and campus counseling resources and ends the session gracefully
212	
213	For full design rationale, see `docs/neurodiversity-agent-design.md`.
214	
215	***
216	
217	## Built By
218	
219	Chauncey O — COS 498: Generative AI Agents, Spring 2026, University of Maine
220

file.content:
# Neurodiversity Support Agent

An AI agent prototype that helps neurodivergent college students turn abstract self-knowledge into concrete situated action. Built as a Claude Code skills-based prototype for COS 498/598: Generative AI Agents, Spring 2026 at the University of Maine.

The agent acts as a collaborative thought partner — warm, specific, and non-patronizing. It helps students decode confusing assignments, apply what they know about how they work, find practical strategies, and recognize burnout before it becomes a crisis.

***

## What This Agent Does

- **Decodes confusing academic situations** — assignment prompts, rubric language, professor feedback, and socially demanding events like presentations or advisor meetings
- **Maps self-knowledge to situated action** — takes what you know about how you work and applies it to the specific challenge in front of you right now
- **Recommends a full strategy toolkit** — prioritizes non-AI approaches like body-doubling, interest-layer reframing, environmental anchoring, and peer strategies
- **Checks for burnout** — helps you recognize masking fatigue and over-accommodation, and treats rest as a legitimate option, not a last resort

***

## Web Version

- Access the web version of the Agent at https://co-4-2-prototype-class-project-agent-production.up.railway.app/
- If you wish to run the agent on your own device instead, continue below.

## Prerequisites

- VS Code with the Claude Code extension installed and authenticated
- An Anthropic API key or Claude Max subscription
- This repository cloned locally and opened in VS Code
- Entire CLI enabled (`entire enable` from the repo root in WSL/terminal)

***

## Setup

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/ChaunceyCon/CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent.git
cd CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent

# Enable Entire (requires WSL or Linux terminal)
entire enable

# Start Claude Code
claude
```

***

## How to Run the Agent

### Step 1: Start a fresh Claude Code session

Open a new terminal session and run:

```bash
claude
```

Make sure **Superpowers plugin is disabled** if you have it installed. Run `/plugin` inside Claude Code and verify it is toggled off. If superpowers is active, it will intercept the skills and the agent will not work correctly.

### Step 2: Paste the starter prompt

Copy and paste the following into Claude Code, replacing the user message in quotes with your own situation:

```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"[Write your opening message here — see example prompts below]"
```

### Step 3: Continue the conversation naturally

After the agent responds, keep replying as yourself. The agent will route between skills automatically based on what you need.

***

## Example Opening Prompts

Try one of these to get started, or write your own:

**If you are confused by an assignment:**
```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"I have a paper due tomorrow and I keep reading the prompt but
I do not understand what the professor actually wants from me."
```

**If you are struggling to start something:**
```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"I have a big assignment this weekend but it feels pointless and
I cannot make myself start it. I know how I work but I do not
know how to apply that here."
```

**If you are exhausted and burning out:**
```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"I am so tired. I have been pushing through everything for weeks
and I do not know how much longer I can keep doing this."
```

**If you want strategies for a specific situation:**
```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"I have a group presentation next week and I am dreading it.
I need strategies that actually fit how I work, not generic advice."
```

***

## Skills Overview

| Skill | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `session-start` | Reads your opening message and routes to the right skill. Asks one clarifying question if needed. |
| `situation-decoder` | Decodes confusing assignments, rubrics, professor feedback, and social situations step by step. |
| `strength-mapper` | Translates what you know about yourself into one concrete adjusted approach for today's challenge. |
| `strategy-toolkit` | Recommends targeted strategies based on your environment, energy, and time. Non-AI approaches first. |
| `burnout-check` | Helps you recognize masking fatigue and burnout. Offers rest as a real option, not a last resort. |

***

## Persona Files

Test personas are available in `data/personas/` to help simulate realistic interactions:

- `maya.json` — Sophomore with ADHD, working two jobs, chronically behind
- `david.json` — Senior CS student, autistic, heavy masker, struggles with ambiguous instructions
- `priya.json` — Junior in nursing, high-achieving but burning out from over-accommodation
- `jordan.json` — Freshman, first-gen, dyslexic, avoids asking for help
- `chauncey.json` — CS junior persona for self-testing

To use a persona in testing, reference the persona name in your opening message (e.g., "I am Maya...") so the agent can read the corresponding file and personalize its responses.

***

## Repository Structure

```
.claude/
  skills/
    session-start/SKILL.md
    situation-decoder/SKILL.md
    strength-mapper/SKILL.md
    strategy-toolkit/SKILL.md
    burnout-check/SKILL.md
data/
  personas/          — JSON persona files for testing
  interactions/
    actual-logs/     — Real test conversation logs
docs/
  neurodiversity-agent-design.md   — Full design document
eval/
  evaluate.py        — Evaluation functions
  run_eval.py        — Evaluation runner script
  sample_conversations.json
  results/           — Per-conversation evaluation output
experiments/
  eval-structural-results.md
  maya-test-1-notes.md
  chauncey-test-1-notes.md
  chauncey-test-2-notes.md
```

***

## Running Evaluations

From the repo root in WSL:

```bash
# Structural metrics only (no API key needed)
python3 eval/run_eval.py --precomputed > experiments/eval-structural-results.md

# Full interactive evaluation
python3 eval/run_eval.py
```

***

## Design Principles

- The agent asks questions rather than lectures
- Non-AI strategies (body-doubling, environmental anchoring, peer approaches) are always offered first
- The agent never diagnoses, labels, or pathologizes the user
- Rest and stopping are treated as legitimate options, not last resorts
- If a user expresses a crisis, the agent provides 988 and campus counseling resources and ends the session gracefully

For full design rationale, see `docs/neurodiversity-agent-design.md`.

***

## Built By

Chauncey O — COS 498: Generative AI Agents, Spring 2026, University of Maine

lastPrompt: I need to update the readme to include info on the SDK package. here is a copy paste of what may be relevant:Prerequisites (both interfaces) Python 3.10 or higher  An Anthropic API key (get one here)…

content.text:
I read the current README and confirmed `coach.py` and `web_coach.py` both exist at the repo root, so the content you pasted maps to real files. Per your standing preference, I want to confirm the plan before editing.

**Proposed changes to `README.md`:**

1. **Replace the current "Prerequisites" section** (lines 23–29) with the shared prerequisites you provided (Python 3.10+, Anthropic API key, repo cloned locally) — including the `git clone` block.

2. **Replace the current "Setup" + "How to Run the Agent" + "Example Opening Prompts" sections** (lines 32–131) with three labeled options:
   - **Option 1 — Web Chat Interface (Flask)**: `pip install flask anthropic`, set API key, `python web_coach.py`, About Me panel instructions.
   - **Option 2 — Claude Agent SDK (Terminal/CLI)**: `pip install claude-agent-sdk anthropic`, set API key, `python coach.py`, plus the two example opening messages you provided.
   - **Option 3 — Original Claude Code Terminal**: keep the existing `claude` workflow and the four verbose starter-prompt examples currently in the README, since they're the working examples for that mode.

3. **Leave untouched**: the top intro, "What This Agent Does", "Web Version" (Railway link stays), "Skills Overview", "Persona Files", "Repository Structure", "Running Evaluations", "Design Principles", "Built By".

A couple of small judgment calls I want you to confirm:
- The four long starter-prompt blocks (lines 84–131) are specific to the Claude Code mode. Keep them under Option 3, or trim them since Options 1 and 2 don't need that prompt-engineering boilerplate? I'd recommend **keep under Option 3**.
- Your pasted content ends with a footer (`KilldroneX killdronex`) and a note about confirming `coach.py` exists — I'll **omit both** from the README.

Want me to proceed with this plan?

Here's the diff preview. Everything outside this block stays as-is.

**Replacing lines 23–131** (current `Prerequisites` → end of `Example Opening Prompts`) with:

```markdown
## Prerequisites

- Python 3.10 or higher
- An Anthropic API key ([get one here](https://console.anthropic.com/))
- This repository cloned locally

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ChaunceyCon/CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent.git
cd CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent
```

***

## Option 1 — Web Chat Interface (Flask)

The web interface runs in your browser at `localhost:5000`.

Install dependencies:

```bash
pip install flask anthropic
```

Set your API key:

```bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key-here
```

Start the server:

```bash
python web_coach.py
```

Open in browser:

```
http://localhost:5000/
```

Use the **About Me** panel (tab on the right side) to enter your name, age, neurodivergent conditions, and background before starting a chat. This lets the coach personalize responses to your situation. Fill it out and click **Save profile** before sending your first message.

***

## Option 2 — Claude Agent SDK (Terminal/CLI)

The CLI version runs directly in your terminal using the Claude Agent SDK.

Install the SDK (Claude Code CLI is bundled automatically — no separate install needed):

```bash
pip install claude-agent-sdk anthropic
```

Set your API key:

```bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key-here
```

Run the coach:

```bash
python coach.py
```

You will be prompted to type your opening message. The agent reads your `.claude/skills/` files and routes to the right skill automatically.

Example opening messages:

```
What's on your mind? → I have a paper due tomorrow and I keep reading the
prompt but I do not understand what the professor actually wants from me.

What's on your mind? → I am so tired. I have been pushing through everything
for weeks and I do not know how much longer I can keep doing this.
```

Type `exit` at any point to end the session.

***

## Option 3 — Original Claude Code Terminal

```bash
# From the repo root in WSL/terminal
claude
```

### Step 1: Start a fresh Claude Code session

Make sure **Superpowers plugin is disabled** if you have it installed. Run `/plugin` inside Claude Code and verify it is toggled off. If superpowers is active, it will intercept the skills and the agent will not work correctly.

### Step 2: Paste the starter prompt

Copy and paste the following into Claude Code, replacing the user message in quotes with your own situation:

```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),

Yes — the existing **Web Version** section (lines 18–21) is outside my replacement range, so it stays exactly as-is:

```markdown
## Web Version

- Access the web version of the Agent at https://co-4-2-prototype-class-project-agent-production.up.railway.app/

That section sits right above Prerequisites, so the flow becomes: hosted site first → "or run it yourself" → Prerequisites → Options 1/2/3.

One small optional tweak you might want: rename that header from `Web Version` to something like `Hosted Version (No Setup Required)` so it's obvious that's the zero-effort path, distinct from Option 1 which is *also* a web interface but locally hosted. input.new_string:
## Hosted Version (No Setup Required)

- Access the web version of the Agent at https://co-4-2-prototype-class-project-agent-production.up.railway.app/
- If you wish to run the agent on your own device instead, continue below.

## Prerequisites

- Python 3.10 or higher
- An Anthropic API key ([get one here](https://console.anthropic.com/))
- This repository cloned locally

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ChaunceyCon/CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent.git
cd CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent
```

***

## Option 1 — Web Chat Interface (Flask)

The web interface runs in your browser at `localhost:5000`.

Install dependencies:

```bash
pip install flask anthropic
```

Set your API key:

```bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key-here
```

Start the server:

```bash
python web_coach.py
```

Open in browser:

```
http://localhost:5000/
```

Use the **About Me** panel (tab on the right side) to enter your name, age, neurodivergent conditions, and background before starting a chat. This lets the coach personalize responses to your situation. Fill it out and click **Save profile** before sending your first message.

***

## Option 2 — Claude Agent SDK (Terminal/CLI)

The CLI version runs directly in your terminal using the Claude Agent SDK.

Install the SDK (Claude Code CLI is bundled automatically — no separate install needed):

```bash
pip install claude-agent-sdk anthropic
```

Set your API key:

```bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key-here
```

Run the coach:

```bash
python coach.py
```

You will be prompted to type your opening message. The agent reads your `.claude/skills/` files and routes to the right skill automatically.

Example opening messages:

```
What's on your mind? → I have a paper due tomorrow and I keep reading the
prompt but I do not understand what the professor actually wants from me.

What's on your mind? → I am so tired. I have been pushing through everything
for weeks and I do not know how much longer I can keep doing this.
```

Type `exit` at any point to end the session.

***

## Option 3 — Original Claude Code Terminal

```bash
# From the repo root in WSL/terminal
claude
```

### Step 1: Start a fresh Claude Code session

Make sure **Superpowers plugin is disabled** if you have it installed. Run `/plugin` inside Claude Code and verify it is toggled off. If superpowers is active, it will intercept the skills and the agent will not work correctly.

### Step 2: Paste the starter prompt

Copy and paste the following into Claude Code, replacing the user message in quotes with your own situation:

```

use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"[Write your opening message here — see example prompts below]"
```

### Step 3: Continue the conversation naturally

After the agent responds, keep replying as yourself. The agent will route between skills automatically based on what you need.

### Example Opening Prompts

Try one of these to get started, or write your own:

**If you are confused by an assignment:**
```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"I have a paper due tomorrow and I keep reading the prompt but
I do not understand what the professor actually wants from me."
```

**If you are struggling to start something:**
```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"I have a big assignment this weekend but it feels pointless and
I cannot make myself start it. I know how I work but I do not
know how to apply that here."
```

**If you are exhausted and burning out:**
```

use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"I am so tired. I have been pushing through everything for weeks
and I do not know how much longer I can keep doing this."
```

**If you want strategies for a specific situation:**
```

use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Neurodiversity Support Agent

An AI agent prototype that helps neurodivergent college students turn abstract self-knowledge into concrete situated action. Built as a Claude Code skills-based prototype for COS 498/598: Generative AI Agents, Spring 2026 at the University of Maine.

The agent acts as a collaborative thought partner — warm, specific, and non-patronizing. It helps students decode confusing assignments, apply what they know about how they work, find practical strategies, and recognize burnout before it becomes a crisis.

***

## What This Agent Does

- **Decodes confusing academic situations** — assignment prompts, rubric language, professor feedback, and socially demanding events like presentations or advisor meetings
- **Maps self-knowledge to situated action** — takes what you know about how you work and applies it to the specific challenge in front of you right now
- **Recommends a full strategy toolkit** — prioritizes non-AI approaches like body-doubling, interest-layer reframing, environmental anchoring, and peer strategies
- **Checks for burnout** — helps you recognize masking fatigue and over-accommodation, and treats rest as a legitimate option, not a last resort

***

## Web Version

- Access the web version of the Agent at https://co-4-2-prototype-class-project-agent-production.up.railway.app/
- If you wish to run the agent on your own device instead, continue below.

## Prerequisites

- VS Code with the Claude Code extension installed and authenticated
- An Anthropic API key or Claude Max subscription
- This repository cloned locally and opened in VS Code
- Entire CLI enabled (`entire enable` from the repo root in WSL/terminal)

***

## Setup

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/ChaunceyCon/CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent.git
cd CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent

# Enable Entire (requires WSL or Linux terminal)
entire enable

# Start Claude Code
claude
```

***

## How to Run the Agent

### Step 1: Start a fresh Claude Code session

Open a new terminal session and run:

```bash
claude
```

Make sure **Superpowers plugin is disabled** if you have it installed. Run `/plugin` inside Claude Code and verify it is toggled off. If superpowers is active, it will intercept the skills and the agent will not work correctly.

### Step 2: Paste the starter prompt

Copy and paste the following into Claude Code, replacing the user message in quotes with your own situation:

```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"[Write your opening message here — see example prompts below]"
```

### Step 3: Continue the conversation naturally

After the agent responds, keep replying as yourself. The agent will route between skills automatically based on what you need.

***

## Example Opening Prompts

Try one of these to get started, or write your own:

**If you are confused by an assignment:**
```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"I have a paper due tomorrow and I keep reading the prompt but
I do not understand what the professor actually wants from me."
```

**If you are struggling to start something:**
```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"I have a big assignment this weekend but it feels pointless and
I cannot make myself start it. I know how I work but I do not
know how to apply that here."
```

**If you are exhausted and burning out:**
```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"I am so tired. I have been pushing through everything for weeks
and I do not know how much longer I can keep doing this."
```

**If you want strategies for a specific situation:**
```
Using only the skills available to you (YOU MUST USE THE SKILLS),
use the session-start skill to respond to the following prompt
from a user of the AI agent system this project prototypes.
YOU MAY ONLY USE THE SKILLS AND MAY NOT READ ANY OTHER FILES
TO PREPARE OR DECIDE ON A RESPONSE.

"I have a group presentation next week and I am dreading it.
I need strategies that actually fit how I work, not generic advice."
```

***

## Skills Overview

| Skill | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `session-start` | Reads your opening message and routes to the right skill. Asks one clarifying question if needed. |
| `situation-decoder` | Decodes confusing assignments, rubrics, professor feedback, and social situations step by step. |
| `strength-mapper` | Translates what you know about yourself into one concrete adjusted approach for today's challenge. |
| `strategy-toolkit` | Recommends targeted strategies based on your environment, energy, and time. Non-AI approaches first. |
| `burnout-check` | Helps you recognize masking fatigue and burnout. Offers rest as a real option, not a last resort. |

***

## Persona Files

Test personas are available in `data/personas/` to help simulate realistic interactions:

- `maya.json` — Sophomore with ADHD, working two jobs, chronically behind
- `david.json` — Senior CS student, autistic, heavy masker, struggles with ambiguous instructions
- `priya.json` — Junior in nursing, high-achieving but burning out from over-accommodation
- `jordan.json` — Freshman, first-gen, dyslexic, avoids asking for help
- `chauncey.json` — CS junior persona for self-testing

To use a persona in testing, reference the persona name in your opening message (e.g., "I am Maya...") so the agent can read the corresponding file and personalize its responses.

***

## Repository Structure

```
.claude/
  skills/
    session-start/SKILL.md
    situation-decoder/SKILL.md
    strength-mapper/SKILL.md
    strategy-toolkit/SKILL.md
    burnout-check/SKILL.md
data/
  personas/          — JSON persona files for testing
  interactions/
    actual-logs/     — Real test conversation logs
docs/
  neurodiversity-agent-design.md   — Full design document
eval/
  evaluate.py        — Evaluation functions
  run_eval.py        — Evaluation runner script
  sample_conversations.json
  results/           — Per-conversation evaluation output
experiments/
  eval-structural-results.md
  maya-test-1-notes.md
  chauncey-test-1-notes.md
  chauncey-test-2-notes.md
```

***

## Running Evaluations

From the repo root in WSL:

```bash
# Structural metrics only (no API key needed)
python3 eval/run_eval.py --precomputed > experiments/eval-structural-results.md

# Full interactive evaluation
python3 eval/run_eval.py
```

***

## Design Principles

- The agent asks questions rather than lectures
- Non-AI strategies (body-doubling, environmental anchoring, peer approaches) are always offered first
- The agent never diagnoses, labels, or pathologizes the user
- Rest and stopping are treated as legitimate options, not last resorts
- If a user expresses a crisis, the agent provides 988 and campus counseling resources and ends the session gracefully

For full design rationale, see `docs/neurodiversity-agent-design.md`.

***

## Built By

Chauncey O — COS 498: Generative AI Agents, Spring 2026, University of Maine

lines: - Access the web version of the Agent at https://co-4-2-prototype-class-project-agent-production.up.railway.app/
- If you wish to run the agent on your own device instead, continue below.
-- VS Code with the Claude Code extension installed and authenticated
-- An Anthropic API key or Claude Max subscription
-- This repository cloned locally and opened in VS Code
-- Entire CLI enabled (`entire enable` from the repo root in WSL/terminal)
+- An Anthropic API key ([get one here](https://console.anthropic.com/))
+The web interface runs in your browser at `localhost:5000`.
-# Enable Entire (requires WSL or Linux terminal)

+Use the **About Me** panel (tab on the right side) to enter your name, age, neurodivergent conditions, and background before starting a chat. This lets the coach personalize responses to your situation. Fill it out and click **Save profile** before sending your first message.
-### Step 1: Start a fresh Claude Code session
+The CLI version runs directly in your terminal using the Claude Agent SDK.
-Open a new terminal session and run:
+Install the SDK (Claude Code CLI is bundled automatically — no separate install needed):
+You will be prompted to type your opening message. The agent reads your `.claude/skills/` files and routes to the right skill automatically.
+What's on your mind? → I have a paper due tomorrow and I keep reading the
+prompt but I do not understand what the professor actually wants from me.
+What's on your mind? → I am so tired. I have been pushing through everything
+for weeks and I do not know how much longer I can keep doing this.
+Type `exit` at any point to end the session.
+# From the repo root in WSL/terminal
+### Step 1: Start a fresh Claude Code session

Make sure **Superpowers plugin is disabled** if you have it installed. Run `/plugin` inside Claude Code and verify it is toggled off. If superpowers is active, it will intercept the skills and the agent will not work correctly.
After the agent responds, keep replying as yourself. The agent will route between skills automatically based on what you need.
